nyheter.title: Taiwan Receives Latest Coast Guard Cutter from $1.3B Fleet Build-up Plan
nyheter.summary: Taipei received its latest cutter this week from a $1.3 billion program initiated almost a decade ago to expand Taiwan’s white hull fleet to counter coercive Chinese activities. Taiwan’s Coast Guard Administration (CGA) took delivery of the 1,000-ton patrol ship Penghu (CG 1007) from the CSBC Corporation’s Keelung shipyard on Tuesday. nyheter.title: VIDEO: Decommissioned USS Mobile Bay, USS Peleliu Sunk in RIMPAC Drills
nyheter.summary: Two decommissioned warships, cruiser USS Mobile Bay (CG-53) amphibious assault ship USS Peleliu (LHA-5), were recently sunk off the coast of Hawaii as part of the U.S. Navy-led Rim of the Pacific 2026 exercise. Mobile Bay was sunk July 11 and Peleliu was sunk July 17 by forces from the U.S. and partner nations in sinking exercises, according to a U.S. Navy news release published Wednesday. nyheter.title: Amphib USS Tripoli Wraps 6-month Indo-Pacific, Middle East Patrol
nyheter.summary: Big deck amphibious ship USS Tripoli (LHA-7), lead ship of the Tripoli Amphibious Ready Group, wrapped up on Wednesday a six-month patrol that took the group to the Indo-Pacific and Middle East. Tripoli pulled into White Beach Naval Facility, Okinawa, on Wednesday to offload elements of the 31st Marine Expeditionary Unit. nyheter.title: Republican Senators Introduce Bill to Create Service Secretary for the Coast Guard
nyheter.summary: A trio of Republican senators have introduced a bill to create Secretary of the Coast Guard and Undersecretary positions as the civilian head of the 43,000-member service, USNI News has learned. According to the draft ‘‘Coast Guard Leadership Modernization Act’’ obtained by USNI News, the new Secretary of the Coast Guard position would report to the Secretary of Homeland Security and takeover many of the interactions the service had with the Pentagon, according to the bill text. nyheter.title: Philippine Sailor Injured in Violent Clash with China Coast Guard off Second Thomas Shoal
nyheter.summary: MANILA, PHILIPPINES – A Philippine sailor was injured today at Second Thomas Shoal following a clash with a Chinese small boat at the contested South China Sea maritime feature. The melee resulted in a blow to the head of a Philippine service member, according to photos and video released from the incident by the Armed Forces of the Philippines (AFP). nyheter.summary: Hanwha Philly Shipyard will build a new missile-range instrumentation vessel for the U.S. Navy that will support the Trump administration’s Golden Dome effort, White House Office of Management and Budget Director Russ Vought announced Friday. nyheter.title: Spanish Navy to Lead NATO’s Atlantic Task Force Until June 2027
nyheter.summary: The Spanish Navy has assumed command of NATO’s naval force dedicated to patrolling the North Atlantic and will lead the force for the next year, NATO announced Tuesday. From July of 2026 to June of 2027, NATO’s Commander Task Force Atlantic (CTF-AT) will be led by five Spanish ships, the Spanish Marine Corps landing force and eight ships from allied nations.
